paragraph: Hackensack's market breaks into four distinct property types, each with its own rhythm. Single-family homes are moving at a median of $630,000 with a 23.5-day median days on market and over 60 percent of sales closing above asking, which signals a competitive but balanced seller's market where priced homes are moving steadily. Multi-family properties sit higher at a $787,500 median and move even faster at 19 days on market, also with 60 percent selling above list, suggesting slightly tighter competition in this tier. Condos and townhouses are the real volume, with 105 sales over eight months at a $320,000 median. These properties tick at 28 days on market and only 35 percent over asking, making this the most buyer-friendly tier where you'll find the most inventory and negotiating room. Co-ops round out the market at a $200,000 median with 13 sales and a sale-to-list ratio below 1.0, meaning homes are selling slightly below asking. The deciding factor isn't market heat across tiers. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family or multi-family property, the lower price point and smaller footprint of a condo, or the entry-level affordability of a co-op.
